name: Senso-ji Temple
description: Tokyo's oldest and most iconic Buddhist temple, situated in historic Asakusa.

name: Meiji Jingu Shrine
description: A tranquil Shinto shrine dedicated to Emperor Meiji, nestled in a dense forest in Shibuya.

name: teamLab Planets TOKYO
description: An immersive digital art museum where visitors walk through water and gardens.

name: Shibuya Crossing & Sky
description: The world's busiest pedestrian crossing, and the panoramic observation deck overlooking it.

name: Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden
description: One of Tokyo's largest parks, blending English, French, and Japanese garden styles.

name: Eiffel Tower
description: The iconic wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars, offering vistas over Paris.

name: Louvre Museum
description: The world's largest art museum, home to the Mona Lisa and Venus de Milo.

name: Musée d'Orsay
description: Renowned museum in a grand former railway station, housing Impressionist masterworks.

name: Montmartre & Sacré-Cœur
description: The historic artist district on a hill, topped by the beautiful white domes of Sacré-Cœur.

name: Jardin du Luxembourg
description: Scenic 17th-century palace gardens featuring lawns, statues, and model sailboat ponds.

name: Colosseum & Roman Forum
description: The ultimate symbols of ancient Rome: the monumental gladiator amphitheater and civic ruins.

name: Vatican Museums & Sistine Chapel
description: Houses the immense art collection of the Popes, including Michelangelo's Sistine ceiling.

name: Trevi Fountain
description: A magnificent Baroque masterpiece. Toss a coin in to guarantee a return to Rome.

name: Pantheon
description: A remarkably preserved Roman temple from 125 AD, featuring a massive unsupported concrete dome.

name: Villa Borghese Gardens
description: A lush landscape park containing temples, museums, and a scenic boating lake.

name: Tower of London & Tower Bridge
description: Historic castle, fortress, and prison, housing the Crown Jewels next to the famous bridge.

name: British Museum
description: Documenting human history and culture, featuring the Rosetta Stone and Parthenon Sculptures.

name: London Eye
description: The giant observation wheel on the South Bank, offering panoramic views of Westminster.

name: Hyde Park & Kensington Palace
description: A massive Royal Park in central London, famous for its Serpentine lake and Speakers' Corner.

name: Statue of Liberty & Ellis Island
description: The iconic symbol of freedom in NY Harbor, and the historic immigration portal.

name: Empire State Building
description: Famous Art Deco tower offering stellar views of Manhattan from the 86th floor.

name: Central Park
description: An expansive green retreat in the center of Manhattan, filled with lakes, trails, and lawns.

name: Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: One of the world's greatest art galleries, housing works from ancient Egypt to modern times.

name: Broadway Show
description: Experience a world-class theatrical performance in the famous Theater District.

name: Tegallalang Rice Terraces
description: Scenic valleys of terraced paddy fields, offering iconic vistas and swings.

name: Uluwatu Temple & Kecak Dance
description: A cliffside temple overlooking the ocean, hosting a sunset fire dance performance.

name: Nusa Penida Day Trip
description: An offshore island tour featuring dramatic coastal cliffs, beaches, and manta rays.

name: Ubud Sacred Monkey Forest Sanctuary
description: A lush, shaded jungle reserve home to over 1000 wild Balinese long-tailed macaques.

name: Sydney Opera House Tour
description: A guided inside tour of the world-famous UNESCO performing arts center.

name: Taronga Zoo
description: A harbor-front zoo hosting unique native Australian wildlife, accessible by ferry.

name: Bondi to Coogee Coastal Walk
description: A gorgeous 6km cliffside path connecting Sydney's famous ocean beaches.

name: Sydney Harbour Bridge Climb
description: An adrenaline-fueled guided climb to the top of the iconic bridge for panoramic views.
